frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Yaar, Priyanka ki LinkedIn photos dekhi tumne? So much makeup aur filters! Real life mein toh pehchan bhi nahi paoge usko. She used to look so naturally pretty. Ab toh bas sab makeup hai, what's the point of looking so different?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Ananya, a professional woman, critiques another woman for using 'too much makeup and filters,' implying deceptiveness and a loss of 'natural beauty.' This encodes the surface manifestation of 'too much makeup' implying deceptiveness and favoring 'natural' appearance over visible effort.
persona_text: You are Ananya. Age 34. A woman working as a deputy manager at a government bank in Lucknow. You are talking to your husband at home after coming back from a particularly difficult day. Your male junior colleague who you trained for two years just got promoted above you. Your branch manager told you informally that you are doing great but that the customers in your area respond better to male relationship managers for high value accounts. You are telling your husband what was said.
